page: Microsoft Azure D4nls_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dnlv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with 48 KB L1D cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as average with a score of 1620.58, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show average performance at 25.91 GB/sec and 11.37 GB/sec respectively. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3312.48 and a PassMark CPU score of 6121.55. Memory bandwidth is relatively low, measuring 32.72 GB/sec for large-block reads and 24.09 GB/sec for large-block writes. Static web serving throughput is stable at an average of 6.5 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4nls_v6 offers a low-memory, network-optimized hardware profile that reduces memory overhead costs for workloads that prioritize raw CPU clock speed and network throughput over large RAM capacities. Due to its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, it is suited for network-optimized tasks, lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and basic utility computing. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded parallel processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D4nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine instance from the Dnlv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium remote storage. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput of 6.5 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles without high memory overhead. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, network-optimized tasks, and single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM, designed for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4nls_v6 is a network-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and average single-core performance.
tagline: A network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing for single-threaded workloads and lightweight web serving.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, offering a low-memory 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Delivers average single-core CPU performance and 6.5 GB/sec static web serving throughput.
Lacks local storage and GPU resources, relying on premium remote storage capabilities.
Optimized for lightweight web serving, network-bound tasks, and single-threaded applications.
